name: Leadership and Aerospace Studies Minor

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: No plan-of-study or requirement groups are parsed at all (requirementGroups=0), so the minor presents no ordered sequence. The visible course list is a grab-bag that mixes the genuine AFROTC leadership ladder (AS 333 Leading People & Effective Communication -> AS 334 -> AS 443 National Security Affairs -> AS 444 Preparation for Active Duty) with unrelated courses that clearly bled in from adjacent catalog pages (BIOL 115, BIOL 150, MATH 107/108/110/111/138, THTR 480/483/484), leaving no coherent progression a student could follow.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish an explicit plan of study listing the required Aerospace Studies course sequence (AS 100 through AS 444) and remove the unrelated BIOL/MATH/THTR courses that do not belong to the minor.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The AS ladder that is present has a sensible depth (deepestPrereqChain=4: AS 333 -> AS 334 -> AS 443 -> AS 444) with clear 'or approval of the Professor of Aerospace Studies' escape clauses, but the entire foundational spine dangles: all 9 missing references (missingCoursesSample: AS 100, AS 111, AS 112, AS 200, AS 221, AS 222, AS 300, AS 400, GER 200) are the core AFROTC courses the upper AS courses build on, so the prerequisite graph has no valid base in the catalog.
dimensions.suggestion: Add catalog entries for the AS 100/111/112/200/221/222/300/400 core so the prerequisite chain resolves rather than dangling at its foundation.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives appear in the data. This is especially notable for a leadership minor, whose entire purpose is developing named leadership and officership competencies that should be straightforward to articulate.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish explicit learning outcomes (e.g., the leadership, communication, and national-security competencies a completer demonstrates) on the catalog page.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: There is no credit accounting whatsoever: statedTotalCredits is null, summedTermCredits is null, and creditReconcileDelta is null with zero requirement groups. Null reconciliation is an expected data-consistency caveat for a minor, but here a prospective student cannot see how many credits or courses the minor requires at all, and the labeled leadership-lab entries (AS 335/336) carry 0 credits.
dimensions.suggestion: State the minor's total credit requirement and the specific credit-bearing courses that count toward it.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page has no term structure, several core AS entries have empty descriptions (AS 301, AS 335, AS 336, AS 401) and truncated titles ('Leading People & Effective Com', 'Aeropsace Independent Study' [sic]), and unrelated courses appear alongside the AS content, making the entry read as a raw extract rather than a curated minor.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the page with labeled requirements, complete AS course descriptions, and corrected titles/spelling, and drop the non-AS courses.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: As an Air Force ROTC commissioning-track minor, the present content is appropriate to its professional purpose: leadership and communication (AS 333/334), national security affairs (AS 443), and preparation for active duty (AS 444) are standard Professional Officer Course material, so 'industry currency' maps to current military-officer development rather than a technical field. What is dated is the framing: the visible national-security content does not yet reflect the newer operational domains (space, cyber) that now define Air and Space Force careers.
dimensions.suggestion: Refresh AS 443 National Security Affairs to foreground space-domain and cyber operations and joint all-domain command themes so the minor mirrors current Air and Space Force doctrine.

descriptionQuality.justification: Description quality is uneven: several core AS courses have empty descriptionExcerpt fields (AS 301, AS 335, AS 336, AS 401) and titles are truncated or misspelled, while only AS 333/334/443/444 carry substantive text, so a reader cannot tell what much of the minor covers.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: For the courses that are present the prerequisites are stated clearly and consistently (each upper AS course lists the prior AS course 'or approval of the Professor of Aerospace Studies'), but because the entire AS 100-400 base is missing from the catalog (missingCourseCount=9), the stated prerequisites point at courses a reader cannot look up.

missingTopics.rationale: AS 443 National Security Affairs covers traditional military-society issues but no visible course addresses the space or cyber domains that now anchor Air and Space Force officer careers.
The leadership sequence (AS 333/334) emphasizes management and communication but no dedicated ethics/officership-values course appears, a core commissioning competency.
AS 444 Preparation for Active Duty prepares officers for service, yet there is no explicit joint/allied operations content reflecting how modern missions are executed.
The management/communication focus of AS 333/334 would be strengthened by explicit content on leading diverse teams, a stated priority across the modern services.

emergingTrends.trend: Space Force and the space domain
emergingTrends.rationale: With the Space Force now a distinct service, the aerospace-studies content built around AS 443/444 should position cadets for space-domain careers, not just air.
National-security content in AS 443 should incorporate cyber and information-operations threats that dominate current defense planning.
Drone and autonomous-platform operations are reshaping air power and warrant coverage in the leadership/national-security sequence.
The leadership courses (AS 333/334) can modernize toward data-driven decision-making and decision-support tools now standard in military command.

topSuggestions: Publish an actual plan of study with the required AS course sequence and a stated credit total; the entry currently shows no requirement groups and no credits.
Add catalog entries for the missing AS 100/111/112/200/221/222/300/400 core so the prerequisite chain no longer dangles at its foundation.
Remove the unrelated BIOL/MATH/THTR courses and fill the empty AS course descriptions (AS 301/335/336/401) so the page reads as a curated minor.
Publish explicit leadership and officership learning outcomes for the minor.
Modernize the national-security and leadership content (AS 443/444) toward space, cyber, and joint all-domain operations.
